title: Stars Eating Planets: A Space Mystery
summary: Scientists have discovered that old stars might be eating giant planets that orbit very close to them. When stars like our Sun get old, they run out of fuel and grow bigger, becoming red giants. This study looked at many stars and found that planets close to these red giants are often missing, which suggests the stars are destroying them. The researchers think this happens because of gravity pulling the planets inward, like a tug-of-war. This could happen to our solar system in billions of years when the Sun becomes a red giant, but Earth is safer because it is farther away. The study helps us understand what happens to planets as stars age.

keywords.explanation: big balls of gas in space that give light and heat
large objects that orbit stars, like Earth
old stars that have grown bigger and cooler
a force that pulls objects toward each other
the path a planet takes around a star
our Sun and all the planets around it

questions.question: What do old stars become when they grow bigger?
What might be happening to planets close to red giants?
What force pulls planets toward stars in this study?
How does the study suggest planets are destroyed?
What will our Sun become in billions of years?
Why is Earth safer than the giant planets in the study?
options: It is farther from the Sun
questions.correct_answer: It is farther from the Sun

background_read: Stars are like big lamps in space that burn fuel. When they get old, they can change and affect planets nearby. This article talks about how scientists used telescopes to watch many stars and planets to learn what happens as stars age. It helps us think about the future of our own solar system.
Article_Structure: WHO: Astronomers from UCL and the University of Warwick
WHAT: Discovered that old stars might be destroying giant planets close to them
WHERE: In space around stars that have become red giants
WHY: To understand what happens to planets as stars age and what might happen to our solar system

perspectives.description: Astronomers found evidence that red giants can pull in and destroy nearby planets.
